meta.title: OpenCairn — AI Knowledge OS
meta.description: Turn files, notes, and research sources into a grounded workspace wiki with evidence, graph views, and reviewable AI actions. Self-hostable · AGPLv3 + commercial license.
meta.ogDescription: OpenCairn turns files and notes into a grounded wiki with Q&A, graph views, and reviewable AI actions.

hero.label: HERO
hero.labelMeta: 2026.04 · SEOUL · AI KNOWLEDGE OS · DUAL LICENSE
hero.sub: Drop in papers, documents, notes, and supported media. OpenCairn builds a workspace wiki, surfaces source-backed links, and keeps AI changes reviewable as workflow surfaces mature.

items.text: Evidence candidates found inside the current project
Run status and outputs recorded in Workflow Console
Generated file linked as a project object

livePanel.title: opencairn — project workflow
rotating: Review: human approval required before apply
Apply: approved change written to the Yjs note
Console: run status and output visible
Recover: cancel and retry state written to ledger

pipeline.label: PIPELINE
pipeline.sub: Instead of one giant prompt, narrow-responsibility steps parse, embed, draft, link, and serve the knowledge base. Answers and graph surfaces carry source chips and evidence panels.

steps.title: Intake
steps.body: PDFs, common documents, Markdown/CSV ZIP exports, Google Drive file-ID imports, and supported media. OCR and media handling depend on provider capability and deployment settings.

steps.title: Break down
steps.body: Text, table structure, and OCR output are normalized to Markdown. Media transcription runs through configured provider paths.

steps.title: Meaning
steps.body: Mapped to vector space. Similar things end up close.

steps.title: Propose
steps.body: The agent drafts typed actions such as note updates, file creation, and document generation.

steps.title: Review
steps.body: Risky changes pass through previews and approvals before they change data.

steps.title: Track
steps.body: Results, errors, and recovery state remain visible in Workflow Console and the action ledger.

citation.label: CITATION
citation.title: Answers and graph views trace back to source evidence.
citation.body: AI answers carry source chips, while graph, card, and mindmap surfaces expose evidence panels. Sentence-level runtime verification is not yet enforced across every writer path.

agents.label: WORKFLOWS
agents.sub: The current product exposes workflow-backed jobs, action ledgers, note actions, generated files, and staged plan/code surfaces. The public roadmap and feature registry remain the source of truth for what is active.

items.name: Source processing
items.body: Uploads and imports become source notes, normalized text, and retrievable project context.

items.name: Grounded Q&A
items.body: Chat and retrieval surfaces answer from scoped workspace context and carry source evidence.

items.name: Note actions
items.body: Create, rename, move, delete, restore, and update flows run through preview-and-apply contracts.

items.name: Generated files
items.body: Project-file and document-generation surfaces keep outputs traceable to workflow runs.

items.name: Action ledger
items.body: Workflow activity, recovery state, and approvals remain visible instead of disappearing into background jobs.

items.name: Plan and code surfaces
items.body: Planning, code workspace, and execution-loop features are staged behind product contracts and feature flags.

views.label: VIEWS
views.title: One graph, five lenses.
views.sub: One dataset, five ways to see it. Switch to whichever view fits the moment. Every switch is logged and reversible.

items.name: Graph
items.body: Explore the full network at a glance

items.name: Mindmap
items.body: Radiate outward from a central concept

items.name: Board
items.body: Collect related cards in one working board

try.label: TRY IT
try.title2: and its evidence and outputs surface.
try.sub: A mock project graph showing how sources, notes, agent actions, generated files, and workflow state connect inside one project. Hover or tap a node to inspect the path.
bullets: Each node is a source, note, action, or output inside a project.
Edges represent evidence, approval, generation, and recovery links.
Counts reflect workflow references and backlinks.
try.caption: Project graph · 9 nodes · 9 edges

attention.d: Uploaded files are parsed and normalized into source notes that can be retrieved.

transformer.d: Yjs-backed notes are the working surface; agent edits are reviewed against the live document.

multihead.d: Chat and actions use scoped evidence candidates and source metadata.

positional.d: The agent creates a diff preview instead of writing directly to the note.

encoder.d: Requests, statuses, results, and error codes are stored for audit and recovery.

decoder.d: Import, generation, code runs, and agent actions share one visible status surface.

bert.d: Documents and code outputs stay as project objects with workflow provenance.

rope.d: Approved changes apply only after checking the Yjs state vector; stale previews are rejected.

pretraining.d: Failed runs can move to cancel, retry, or manual-review paths.

workspace.label: WORKSPACE
workspace.title1: Same shape for a solo user
workspace.sub: Wiki pages aren't just text — they're the meeting point of sources, teammates, and reviewable AI work. Every page carries its citations, editors, and workflow history with it.

mock.body: OpenCairn reads sources, retrieves scoped evidence inside the current project, and turns note or file changes into reviewable actions. Important changes move through preview and approval before they apply.
mock.calloutBody: The agent drafted an update for this paragraph. Review the current note and proposed diff before applying it.

reviewCard.title: Note update proposal
reviewCard.current: Read sources and add a summary to the related note.
reviewCard.draft: Read the source, find evidence candidates in the current project, draft a note update, and leave it as a preview card for human approval.
reviewCard.warning: If the note changes before apply, OpenCairn treats the preview as stale and asks for a fresh one.

feed.text: candidate edge: Source Evidence ↔ Action Ledger
approved change applied to the Yjs note

principles.label: PRINCIPLES
principles.sub: Data sovereignty · living knowledge · one throughline to learning · open source. Tools that keep one or two of these are common. Keeping all four at once — that's OpenCairn.

items.title: Your data stays on your server.
items.body: Text, vectors, and original files all live in your infrastructure. With an Ollama-based local LLM configuration, model calls do not leave your deployment. It can also be run without external connectors.

items.title: AI does the sorting, you do the judging.
items.body: AI actions can draft pages, propose updates, and link them to the existing wiki. You review and edit before important changes land. The time you spent copying by hand disappears.

items.title: Capture → explore → learn, one continuous line.
items.body: Q&A · graph surfaces · review cards · Socratic dialogue · document-generation surfaces — all fed from the same wiki. No tool-hopping.

items.title: Dual-licensed; exit always available.
items.body: Default is AGPLv3 — fork, modify, deploy yourself whenever you want. A separate commercial license is available for organizations that can't take an AGPL component. No risk of the service disappearing.

personas.label: WHO
personas.sub: Linearly scales from an individual to a lab to a regulated enterprise — without swapping tools.

items.title: Grad student · researcher
items.body: Papers and documents become one woven wiki. Explore relationships in the concept graph, ask grounded questions, and self-check before exams with AI quizzes. Citations lead back to your original notes.

items.title: Lab · small team
items.body: Run it with role-based permissions and live collaboration. Keep meeting notes and research sources in one project wiki, so knowledge stays behind when people move on.

items.title: Regulated industries
items.body: Designed with finance, healthcare, government, and universities in mind — places where external SaaS can be restricted. Fully-local LLM paths and commercial licensing can support stricter deployment policies.
bullets: · Audit and security requirements by design

install.label: INSTALL
install.sub: Docker Compose brings up the database, file storage, and workflow engine. Run the app through the development wrapper or self-host profiles; fully-local LLM mode can be configured with the Ollama profile and OpenAI-compatible settings.
bullets: Minimum 4 vCPU · 8GB RAM, recommended 8 vCPU · 16GB RAM
x86_64 + ARM64 multi-arch (Raspberry Pi · Apple Silicon · Oracle Ampere)
pg_dump-based backup, restore, and verification scripts included
terminalLines: # set required secrets and your preferred model provider in .env
$ docker compose up -d postgres redis minio temporal

secondary.label: OTHER ROUTES
secondary.title: Run it yourself, or on-prem.
secondary.sub: If managed cloud isn't for you, here are two more paths.

selfHost.title: Run it yourself with Docker
selfHost.body: Full open-source code (AGPLv3, with separate commercial license available) · self-operated workspaces and members · fully-local LLM option. Hardware and ops are on you.

enterprise.title: On-prem · auth · audit path
enterprise.body: Dedicated install and support path for finance, healthcare, government, and universities — environments where external SaaS can be restricted. Fully-local LLM configuration · organization auth integration design · commercial license inquiry.

items.q: Can OpenCairn analyze audio or video?
items.a: Yes when the supported media path is configured for your deployment. Audio and video are transcribed through provider and worker settings, then handled like text sources. Real-time transcription is not supported; files are processed after upload.

All of it in infrastructure you operate. Text, vectors, and wiki pages live in the database; original files live in file storage. In Gemini mode, AI calls go to the external provider. With an Ollama-based local LLM configuration, model calls do not leave your deployment. External connectors such as Google Drive or Notion only contact those services when enabled.

items.q: Doesn't AGPLv3 mean my company can't use it?
items.a: OpenCairn is dual-licensed. The default AGPLv3 can be evaluated for internal use, but network service models and source-disclosure obligations should go through your legal review. A separate commercial license is available for organizations whose policy disallows AGPL components or source disclosure.

The default paths are Gemini and Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible gateways can also be configured. Feature coverage varies by provider; Gemini-specific capabilities only work when that provider path is enabled.

items.q: Can I migrate from my existing note app?
items.a: Upload a Markdown/CSV export ZIP and OpenCairn rebuilds the folder and note structure into a wiki. Google Drive currently supports file-ID import. App-connected imports, including Notion, belong in the connector roadmap.

items.q: What if the AI gets the wiki wrong?
items.a: AI-generated changes go through review-and-apply flows, and you can edit them afterward. Note changes are tracked through versions and activity records; duplicate and contradiction review continues to expand through workflow-backed surfaces.

items.q: How smooth is the real-time collaboration?
items.a: OpenCairn uses Hocuspocus and Yjs for synchronization. Collaboration surfaces such as cursors, comments, @mentions, notifications, and public links are implemented; real-world smoothness still depends on network and Hocuspocus deployment settings.

cta.label: CLOSING
cta.sub: Start free on Solo today. How much your knowledge base has grown a year from now depends on what you start accumulating today.
cta.bottomTonight: Tonight, start with your first source.

footer.tagline: AI knowledge OS. Solo or team. Self-hosted, forever.
